children.heading: Limitation
children.label: a Limitation
children.text: The total amount of the credit taken under section 901(a) shall not exceed the same proportion of the tax against which such credit is taken which the taxpayer’s taxable income from sources without the United States (but not in excess of the taxpayer’s entire taxable income) bears to his entire taxable income for the same taxable year.

children.heading: Taxable income for purpose of computing limitation
children.label: b Taxable income for purpose of computing limitation

children.heading: Personal exemptions
children.label: 1 Personal exemptions
children.text: For purposes of subsection (a), the taxable income in the case of an individual, estate, or trust shall be computed without any deduction for personal exemptions under section 151 or 642(b).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Taxable income from sources outside the United States shall include gain from the sale or exchange of capital assets only to the extent of foreign source capital gain net income.

children.heading: Special rules where capital gain rate differential
children.label: B Special rules where capital gain rate differential
i
children.text: in lieu of applying subparagraph (A), the taxable income from sources outside the United States shall include gain from the sale or exchange of capital assets only in an amount equal to foreign source capital gain net income reduced by the rate differential portion of foreign source net capital gain,

children.label: ii
children.text: the entire taxable income shall include gain from the sale or exchange of capital assets only in an amount equal to capital gain net income reduced by the rate differential portion of net capital gain, and

children.label: iii
children.text: for purposes of determining taxable income from sources outside the United States, any net capital loss (and any amount which is a short-term capital loss under section 1212(a)) from sources outside the United States to the extent taken into account in determining capital gain net income for the taxable year shall be reduced by an amount equal to the rate differential portion of the excess of net capital gain from sources within the United States over net capital gain.

children.heading: Coordination with capital gains rates
children.label: C Coordination with capital gains rates
children.text: The Secretary may by regulations modify the application of this paragraph and paragraph (3) to the extent necessary to properly reflect any capital gain rate differential under section 1(h) and the computation of net capital gain.

children.heading: Foreign source capital gain net income
children.label: A Foreign source capital gain net income
i
children.text: capital gain net income from sources without the United States, or

children.label: ii
children.text: capital gain net income.

children.heading: Foreign source net capital gain
children.label: B Foreign source net capital gain
i
children.text: net capital gain from sources without the United States, or

children.label: ii
children.text: net capital gain.

children.heading: Section 1231 gains
children.label: C Section 1231 gains
children.text: The term “gain from the sale or exchange of capital assets” includes any gain so treated under section 1231.

children.heading: Capital gain rate differential
children.label: D Capital gain rate differential
children.text: There is a capital gain rate differential for any year if subsection (h) of section 1 applies to such taxable year.

children.label: I
children.text: the highest rate of tax set forth in subsection (a), (b), (c), (d), or (e) of section 1 (whichever applies), over

children.label: II
children.text: the alternative rate of tax determined under section 1(h), bears to

children.label: ii
children.text: that rate referred to in subclause (I).

children.heading: Treatment of dividends for which deduction is allowed under section 245A
children.label: 4 Treatment of dividends for which deduction is allowed under section 245A
A
children.text: the foreign-source portion of any dividend received from such foreign corporation, and

children.label: i
children.text: income (other than amounts includible under section 951(a)(1) or 951A(a)) with respect to stock of such specified 10-percent owned foreign corporation, or

children.label: ii
children.text: such stock to the extent income with respect to such stock is other than amounts includible under section 951(a)(1) or 951A(a).

children.heading: Deductions treated as allocable to foreign source net CFC tested income
children.label: 5 Deductions treated as allocable to foreign source net CFC tested income
A
children.text: any deduction allowed under section 250(a)(1)(B) (and any deduction allowed under section 164(a)(3) for taxes imposed on amounts described in section 250(a)(1)(B)) to such income,

children.label: B
children.text: no amount of interest expense or research and experimental expenditures to such income, and

children.label: C
children.text: any other deduction to such income only if such deduction is directly allocable to such income.

children.heading: Source rules for certain inventory produced in the United States and sold through foreign branches
children.label: 6 Source rules for certain inventory produced in the United States and sold through foreign branches
i
children.text: which is produced in the United States,

children.label: ii
children.text: which is for use outside the United States, and

children.label: iii
children.text: to which the third sentence of section 863(b) applies, and

children.label: B
children.text: is attributable (determined under rules similar to the rules of section 864(c)(5)) to such office or other fixed place of business,

children.heading: Carryback and carryover of excess tax paid
children.label: c Carryback and carryover of excess tax paid
children.text: Any amount by which all taxes paid or accrued to foreign countries or possessions of the United States for any taxable year for which the taxpayer chooses to have the benefits of this subpart exceed the limitation under subsection (a) shall be deemed taxes paid or accrued to foreign countries or possessions of the United States in the first preceding taxable year and in any of the first 10 succeeding taxable years, in that order and to the extent not deemed taxes paid or accrued in a prior taxable year, in the amount by which the limitation under subsection (a) for such preceding or succeeding taxable year exceeds the sum of the taxes paid or accrued to foreign countries or possessions of the United States for such preceding or succeeding taxable year and the amount of the taxes for any taxable year earlier than the current taxable year which shall be deemed to have been paid or accrued in such preceding or subsequent taxable year (whether or not the taxpayer chooses to have the benefits of this subpart with respect to such earlier taxable year). Such amount deemed paid or accrued in any year may be availed of only as a tax credit and not as a deduction and only if the taxpayer for such year chooses to have the benefits of this subpart as to taxes paid or accrued for that year to foreign countries or possessions of the United States. This subsection shall not apply to taxes paid or accrued with respect to amounts described in subsection (d)(1)(A).

children.heading: Separate application of section with respect to certain categories of income
children.label: d Separate application of section with respect to certain categories of income
A
children.text: any amount includible in gross income under section 951A (other than passive category income),

children.label: B
children.text: foreign branch income,

children.label: C
children.text: passive category income, and

children.label: D
children.text: general category income.

children.heading: Passive category income
children.label: i Passive category income
children.text: The term “passive category income” means passive income and specified passive category income.

children.heading: General category income
children.label: ii General category income
children.text: The term “general category income” means income other than income described in paragraph (1)(A), foreign branch income, and passive category income.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general

children.heading: Creditable foreign taxes
children.label: B Creditable foreign taxes
children.text: The term “creditable foreign taxes” means any taxes for which a credit is allowable under section 901; except that such term shall not include any tax unless such tax is shown on a payee statement furnished to such individual.

children.heading: Payee statement
children.label: C Payee statement
children.text: The term “payee statement” has the meaning given to such term by section 6724(d)(2).

children.heading: Estates and trusts not eligible
children.label: D Estates and trusts not eligible
children.text: This subsection shall not apply to any estate or trust.

children.heading: Cross references
children.label: k Cross references
children.text: For increase of limitation under subsection (a) for taxes paid with respect to amounts received which were included in the gross income of the taxpayer for a prior taxable year as a United States shareholder with respect to a controlled foreign corporation, see section 960(c).
